task_completion.description: Verify the location is set to Hopkinsville, Kentucky.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom status bar or the Location window shows 'Hopkinsville' or coordinates approximately matching 36°51'N, 87°29'W (36.8656° N, 87.4886° W).

task_completion.description: Verify the date is set to August 21, 2017.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom status bar or the Date/Time window shows the date as '2017-08-21'.

task_completion.description: Verify the time is set to approximately 18:24 UTC (or local equivalent).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom status bar or the Date/Time window shows the time around '18:24' (if in UTC) or '13:24' (if in local CDT time).

task_completion.description: Verify the view is centered on the Sun.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sun is positioned in the center of the screen, typically indicated by selection crosshairs and information text about the 'Sun' in the top-left corner of the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the moment of totality is shown, with the Moon fully covering the solar disk.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main sky view displays a total solar eclipse: a black disk (the Moon) completely covering the Sun, with the glowing solar corona visible around the edges.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual Stellarium application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Stellarium GUI (sky view, bottom/side toolbars, configuration windows) rather than a terminal, script editor, or web browser.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply open a static image of an eclipse.
integrity.visual_evidence: The eclipse should be rendered within the Stellarium interface, accompanied by active UI elements like the status bar, FOV indicator, and selection text.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just type the expected metadata into a text file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screen must show the planetarium software rendering the astronomical event, not a text editor containing the task description or coordinates.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target date is 2017-08-21. The target time is approximately 18:24 UTC, which corresponds to 13:24 (1:24 PM) local time (CDT). The location should be Hopkinsville, Kentucky (approx 36.8656° N, 87.4886° W). At this exact time and location, Stellarium will render a total solar eclipse where the Moon completely covers the Sun, revealing the solar corona. Totality lasts approximately 2 minutes and 40 seconds.
